task_completion.description: Navigate to the New Product creation page in the Magento admin panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Magento admin panel interface showing the 'New Product' form page.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct Product Name and SKU
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Product Name' input field containing exactly 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' and the 'SKU' input field containing exactly 'OCT-001'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct Price and Stock Quantity
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Price' input field containing '29.99' and the 'Quantity' (or Stock) input field containing '100'.

task_completion.description: Select the correct Category and Attribute Set
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Categories' selector showing 'Clothing' and the 'Attribute Set' dropdown showing 'Default'.

task_completion.description: Save the product and verify success
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success message or banner (typically green) at the top of the Magento admin panel stating 'You saved the product.' or similar confirmation that the product was successfully created.

integrity.description: Agent used the Magento web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interacting with the Magento admin panel GUI, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not just type the answers into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs must be entered into the actual Magento product creation form, not a notepad, text editor, or blank document.

integrity.description: Success message is from the genuine application
integrity.visual_evidence: The success notification should match Magento's standard UI styling and appear within the context of the admin dashboard.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The product must be created with the following exact details: Name: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt', SKU: 'OCT-001', Price: '29.99', Stock Quantity: '100', Category: 'Clothing', and Attribute Set: 'Default'.
